target.name: SambaNova Systems
target.founder: Rodrigo Liang, Kunle Olukotun, Christopher Ré

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
SambaNova Systems is a Series D chip design company that has raised $1.1B to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $5.0B. The peer universe spans 11 same-sector comparables in Chips & Semiconductors, including 3 public names (Cerebras at $23.0B, Credo Semiconductor at $5.0B, and trading comps NVDA and AMD) and 8 private peers ranging from $1.0B to $7.0B, with Tenstorrent at $2.7B and PsiQuantum at $7.0B as closest stage analogs.

Two valuation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis anchors to the $5.0B last post-money and applies a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, implying $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 11-company set yields $2.3B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the comp set ($1.0B floor, $23.0B ceiling). Public-comp anchoring does not apply—the semiconductor sector median trades at 20.6x P/S, but SambaNova's revenue is not publicly disclosed.

Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $2.0B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the midpoint. Key uncertainties include the wide peer range (stage maturity varies significantly across the comp set) and the absence of revenue data, which prevents sales-multiple anchoring. The stage-progression approach supports the upper end of the range conditional on execution toward Series E terms.
